title: Lilly’s Foundayo reaches 1,390 patients in first week, trailing Novo’s oral Wegovy launch
snippet: While Novo Nordisk’s Wegovy pill reached more than 3,000 patients in its first week on the market, analysts at RBC Capital Markets said a direct comparison of the two figures could be misleading given the shorter data collection time for Foundayo.

title: UPDATE: Kailera breaks biotech IPO record with $625M debut
snippet: Obesity-focused Kailera Therapeutics debuted on the Nasdaq Friday after raising a record $625 million, beating Moderna’s $600 million from 2018.

title: RFK Jr. defends Makary, claims pharma ‘owns’ Congress and media
snippet: The pharma industry “own Congress, they own the media,” Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy, Jr. told lawmakers by way of explaining the bad press against FDA Commissioner Marty Makary following the second rejection of Replimune’s advanced melanoma drug.

title: Novo may have muscle advantage over Lilly in weight-loss race: preprint
snippet: More patients on Eli Lilly’s tirzepatide lost over 5% of their lean mass versus those on Novo Nordisk’s semaglutide, according to a study that has yet to be peer reviewed.

title: Trump nominates new CDC director, appoints various leaders to embattled agency
snippet: Erica Schwartz, a former deputy surgeon general and member of the U.S. military, will take over from what has amounted to a rapid succession of CDC leaders over the past year.

title: Drug pricing watchdog calls for increased transparency into FDA’s accelerated approval decisions
snippet: Greater transparency in FDA decision-making was possibly the most common theme for stakeholders interviewed by the Institute for Clinical and Economic Review in a new report focused on strengthening the agency’s accelerated approval program.

title: FDA mulls compounding for peptides previously flagged over safety risks
snippet: With Health Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. declaring himself “a big fan of peptides,” the FDA has arranged an advisory committee to discuss allowing compounders to make molecules despite earlier safety concerns.

title: MeiraGTx, spying hope in a failed trial, buys back eye disease gene therapy from J&J
snippet: J&J will hand over the rights to bota-vec for $25 million upfront, clearing MeiraGTx to seek regulatory approvals in the U.S. and EU in 2027.

title: Lilly’s Foundayo posts ‘outstanding’ diabetes data, possibly easing FDA safety concerns
snippet: Right after the FDA flagged safety concerns for Eli Lilly’s newly approved obesity pill, the pharma posted diabetes data demonstrating a clean safety profile and revealed plans to seek approval in the indication.

title: Can Europe turn world-class science into world-class scale?
snippet: Despite exceptional regional hubs and research strength, investors say Europe still needs more integrated incubators, smarter regulation and broader pools of patient capital to keep breakthrough companies growing at home.

title: Obesity’s explosive growth continues as Q1 deals exceed total 2025 value
snippet: Commitments in obesity- and diabetes-focused deals reached $22 billion in the first quarter of 2026—already eclipsing last year’s total of $20.3 billion, according to a new J.P. Morgan report.

title: Revolution doubles stock offering target to $2B following pancreatic cancer win
snippet: After Revolution Medicines’ drug candidate doubled survival in a Phase 3 pancreatic cancer study, the biotech is hoping to attract more investor money, with new plans for a $2 billion stock and debt offering.

title: Novartis CEO joins Anthropic board, embedding AI in the heart of biopharma

title: Biopharmas pull back on layoffs in Q1
snippet: The trend of fewer companies letting employees go, which began in the fourth quarter of last year, has continued. However, more employees were on the chopping block in Q1 2026 than in Q1 2025, due mainly to one company’s cuts.

snippet: Small cell lung cancer (SCLC) is a highly aggressive malignancy, and chemotherapy frequently causes nausea and vomiting, which can impair treatment tolerance. Because thalidomide (THD) has shown potential clinical benefit in alleviating nausea and anorexia, we investigated whether its effects might be associated with changes in gut microbial composition and...

title: Combining label-free Raman spectroscopy and machine learning to identify early biomarkers of COVID-19 disease severity and mortality.
snippet: Early prediction of COVID-19 severity and mortality is crucial for optimizing clinical care and patient outcomes, but remains challenging. We aim to develop a screening tool combining label-free Raman spectroscopy and machine learning modeling to predict COVID-19 severity and mortality. Patients infected by SARS-CoV-2 () were recruited during the first wave...

title: In Vitro Modulation of Murine Tenocyte Behavior by Hyperbaric Oxygen Therapy.
publicationName: Journal of orthopaedic research : official publication of the Orthopaedic Research Society
journalName: Journal of orthopaedic research : official publication of the Orthopaedic Research Society
snippet: Tendon injuries often result in prolonged healing and excessive scar tissue formation due to chronic hypoxia in this poorly perfused tissue. Hyperbaric oxygen therapy (HBOT), a non-invasive treatment currently used for chronic wounds, may improve tendon healing by relieving hypoxia. title: Eosinophilic Biliary Cystic Neoplasm of the Liver: A Case Report of an Unrecognized Subtype of Intrahepatic Biliary Neoplasm.
snippet: We report a multicystic intrahepatic neoplasm in a 54-year-old Japanese woman, representing a previously unrecognized subtype. Grossly, the lesion was a well-demarcated multicystic tumor with focal papillary projections. Histologically, the cysts were lined by columnar to cuboidal neoplastic cells with brush border-like luminal microvilli, abundant granular...

title: Prasad’s Request To Remain Anonymous Shines Light on FDA’s Transparency Problem
snippet: While requests by government officials for anonymity when speaking to the media are nothing new, the practice attracts more scrutiny when the Department for Health and Human Services has pledged a commitment to “radical transparency.”

title: CAR-T cells in solid tumors: engineering, biomarkers, translational pathways and the road ahead.
snippet: Chimeric antigen receptor (CAR) T-cell therapy has transformed the treatment of hematologic malignancies by enabling antigen-specific tumor targeting and durable clinical responses. However, its translation to solid tumors has been limited by fundamental biological barriers, including antigen heterogeneity, poor tumor infiltration, and profound immunosuppre...

title: KDM5B recruits FOXG1 to suppress the IFN response, leading to malignant progression and immune evasion in cervical cancer.
snippet: Cervical cancer carcinogenesis often exhibits significant heterogeneity, and the molecular mechanisms underlying epithelial cell transformation are not fully understood. title: Single-cell sequencing reveals the functional heterogeneity of melanoma cells and their crosstalk with the tumor microenvironment.
snippet: Cutaneous melanoma is a highly aggressive malignancy characterized by significant heterogeneity, rapid progression, and variable treatment responses. Understanding the functional diversity of melanoma cells and their interactions with the tumor microenvironment (TME) is crucial for developing effective therapeutic strategies and identifying prognostic bioma...

title: Aflatoxin B1 Impairs TACE Efficacy Through Downregulated Carbonic Anhydrase 2: A Bioinformatics Analysis.
snippet: Hepatocellular carcinoma (HCC) is one of the leading causes of cancer-related mortality worldwide, and transcatheter arterial chemoembolization (TACE) remains the core locoregional therapeutic modality for patients with intermediate to advanced disease. title: Advances in dry eye disease: from immunopathological mechanisms to emerging ophthalmic drug delivery systems.
snippet: Dry eye disease (DED) is a multifactorial disorder of the lacrimal functional unit and ocular surface that leads to ocular discomfort, visual disturbance, and tear film instability. It affects a substantial proportion of the global population and is driven by a complex interplay of immune dysregulation, environmental stressors, and systemic factors. Accumul...

title: Macrophage membrane-biomimetic cinnamaldehyde nanomedicine ameliorates inflammatory bowel disease by suppressing macrophage M1 polarization.
snippet: Inflammatory bowel disease (IBD), known for its complexity and frequent relapses, urgently demands novel therapeutics due to the limited efficacy of current treatments.
